publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2021-06-05 18:37:17 UTC.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: Notes, drafts, and writings on jazz criticism and biography. Also, letters to Balliett and photographs.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Whitney Balliett was esteemed jazz critic. He graduated from Cornell in 1951, and went on to write for The New Yorker. For nearly 50 years, he contributed to the publication with over 550 articles, and many more unsigned. He is known for his non-technical approach to criticism, using artful adjectives and vivid verbs to frame the music. His writing was more poetry than anything, and reading it, one will find metaphors and synesthetic synopses understandable by even the most uninformed music listener.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Notes, drafts, and writings on jazz criticism and biography. Also, letters to Balliett and photographs. The Whitney Balliett collection houses a variety of papers and correspondences from Balliett's career. There are a number of notebooks containing (nearly illegible) notes of various musician's concerts or recordings. There are also items relating to the New Yorker, including a drawing of Buddy Rich by Balliett that appeared in the magazine. Some notable letters include notes from Alice Toklas, John Updike, and Frank Sinatra. Additionally, there are a number of formerly framed items from the wall of Balliett's office.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Whitney Balliett papers, #6948. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

did.unittitle: "The Thoughts of Youth" - Poems by Whitney B.
Ghost Experience - Notes about Marian Johnson
Poetry Images... Whitney's Notes for his own Work
American Academy of Artist Letters Program
"Atlantic Monthly" Article on a 10 day QE 2 Jazz Cruise by Whitney Balliett
Bach, Jean (notes on "A Great Day in Harlem")
The Blue Book Guide of the "Sporting District" in New Orleans
Books: James Gould Cozzens Original Manuscript
Book Review: DownBeat - "Ecstasy at the Onion - 31 Pieces on Jazz by Whitney Balliett
Book Review: "God's Perfect Child" by Caroline Fraser
Burns Ken (notes and piece about his Jazz series)
Cain Jackie - Kral, Roy (notes on the singers Jackie + Roy)
Gibson, Dick: Jazz Party - Aspen, Colorado
The Great Hurricane of 1938, Reprint from the New Yorker
"The House and the Highway" - New Yorker
Keating, Cletus (Mrs.) interview for "The House and the Highway"
Monk, Thelonious - Downbeat Score for "Straight No Chaser"
NY Review - E.J. Hobsbawn: Jazz Comes Back
Playing the drums on a QE2 Jazz Cruise
Dates of Whitney Balliett's New Yorker Pieces
Oliver, Edith (original manuscript of Edith Oliver's obituary by Balliett)
Rich, Buddy - Drawing by Balliett which appeared in the New Yorker
Shawn, William (Interview from Womnen's Wear Daily)
Zutty Singleton - Modern Jazz Quartet notes
Letters from Fans of WB's Writing
DownBeat - Letter to Editor - Re: Sidney Catlett (WB)
Letters from: Brian McInerry - Maher, James T.
Wilbur, Bob (letter re: Jelly Roll Morton)
Letters from musicians, writers, critics, and others (including Frank Sinatra)
Reviews and quotes on WB's writing
Lewis, John (memorial service in St. John's Cathedral, letter from a reader)
Letters from Cheatham, Doc - Ellis, Anita - Lewis, John
Photo by Jack Bradley, p. 19 - Whitney, Nancy dancing... from DownBeat magazine
Original Manuscript - City Voices: Fourteen New York Portraits
Photograph of Duke Ellington in front of the Apollo theater, signed to Balliett with appreciation.
Photograph of the members and recipients of awards ceremony of the American Academy of Arts and Letters.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a sheet identifying all people in photograph.

did.unittitle: Harlem 1958 photograph, also known as A Great Day in Harlem, and a modern recreation photograph

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The photograph depicts 57 jazz musicians. In the more modern, recreated photograph at the same location, ten of the original musicians are pictured. The photograph was taken sometime before October 2003.

did.unittitle: Photograph of Ben Webster in silhouette
"Red Allen, Pee Wee Russell Quintet - Introduced by Whitney Balliett" performance poster
Photograph of Balliett with Duke Ellington backstage at the Monterey Jazz Festival.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also in the photograph is Nancy and Fred Seligo's date. Fred took the photograph at three a.m.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photograph of Balliett at home taken by A. Hall, a New Yorker publication with an interview and photographs titled "Whitney Balliett and All That Jazz", and a photograph of drummer Big Sid Catlett by Skippy Adelman taken circa 1945, printed later.

did.unittitle: Pencil drawing on paper of Whitney Balliett playing the drums by Paul Resika
Watercolor and ink drawing for Atlantic Monthly by Barry Blitt.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The illustration was for a piece Balliett wrote about playing the drums with a New Orleans group on a jazz cruise in November 1994, which no critics were invited on. It was a ten day voyage on the QE2 from New York City to Antigua.
